metadata.description: Mental health screening dataset for Indian population — transformer-based text classification
metadata.language_note: Responses reflect Indian English, cultural references (joint family, UPSC, EMI, BPO, festivals, puja rituals, casteism, dowry, etc.), and socioeconomic diversity across urban, semi-urban and rural populations.
cultural_contexts_represented: ADHD in students / housewives / professionals / entrepreneurs
metadata.merge_notes: v4: added 10 new samples per disease (DEP_041-050, ANX_013-022, OCD_013-022, ADHD_013-022). Short colloquial answers with umm/hmm expressions simulating real spoken-style responses. Diverse Indian contexts: accounts clerk OCD, contamination OCD, health anxiety, placement anxiety, parenting anxiety, ADHD in nurse/student/caterer.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: Last month I failed my UPSC prelims for the third time. My parents spent so much money on coaching. I just sat in my room for days, couldn't eat properly. I keep thinking I am a burden on my family and there is no point trying anymore.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I used to wake up at 6 AM for morning walk but now I can't even get out of bed before noon. Simple tasks like bathing or cooking feel like climbing a mountain. I skip meals, just lie on the bed scrolling phone for hours.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I either sleep 12-14 hours or get no sleep at all. My mother makes rotis but I have no appetite. I have lost almost 5 kg in two months. Sometimes I eat nothing the whole day and don't even feel hungry.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I stopped picking up calls from my friends. My father keeps asking me to study but I can't even open the books. I had a fight with my sister because she said I am wasting time. I feel nobody understands me in my own house.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: Earlier I used to plan my whole day, make notes, attend extra classes. Now I have zero motivation. Even watching cricket which I loved feels boring. I feel empty inside, like nothing will ever get better for me.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: My marriage was fixed but the boy's family cancelled because they wanted more dowry. My relatives kept talking about it at every family function. I feel ashamed to go out. I have started thinking no one will ever want to marry me.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I work in a BPO night shift. After shift I come home and just cry. I have no energy to meet friends on weekends. My performance at work has dropped. My team leader gave me a warning last week.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I depend on sleeping tablets since last three months. Without them sleep is impossible. I eat a lot of junk food late at night, gained weight, which makes me feel worse about myself. I skip breakfast and lunch most days.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I haven't visited my parents in Pune in four months. I make excuses. Even video calling them makes me cry because I don't want them to worry. My friends think I am arrogant but actually I am just very sad inside.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I used to do zumba classes, had many hobbies. Now I just exist. I go to work, come back, sleep. There is no joy in anything. Some days I think what is the point of continuing like this.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: My startup failed after two years. I had taken loan from relatives and now I cannot repay. My wife's family is pressuring her. I sit with the loan papers and think there is no way out. I feel like a complete failure.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I was always energetic, used to go to gym. Now even brushing teeth feels like effort. I sit in the same clothes for days. My wife has to push me to eat. I just stare at the ceiling for hours.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I wake up at 3 AM every night with heart racing, thinking about the debt. I cannot go back to sleep. Food has no taste. My mother came from the village and said I look like I am sick.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I shout at my children for small things and then feel guilty. My wife and I barely talk. I refused to attend my cousin's wedding because I couldn't face relatives asking about my business.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I had so many ideas before. Now my mind is blank. I see other entrepreneurs on LinkedIn and feel worthless. I have no desire to try again. Some days I wish I could just disappear.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: My son moved to Canada for job. My husband passed away two years back. Now the house feels very empty and silent. I cry looking at old photos. I feel my purpose in life is over now.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Earlier I used to cook, do puja, go for kitty party. Now I don't feel like doing anything. Some days I don't do puja which I had never missed in 30 years. I watch TV but nothing registers in my mind.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I eat very little. My daughter-in-law calls from Canada and says I look thin on video call. Sleep comes only after midnight and I wake up very early feeling sad. I keep thinking about my husband.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: My neighbor calls me for morning walk but I make excuses. I don't want to burden anyone. In India we say log kya kahenge so I pretend everything is fine but inside I am very lonely and sad all the time.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I used to be very active, organised Ganesh festival in our colony. Now I don't even water my plants properly. My grandchildren video call but I feel I have nothing interesting to say. Life feels meaningless without family around.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I am in 12th standard and my parents keep comparing me to my cousin who got 98% last year. In every family gathering they talk about JEE rank. I scored 72% and now feel I have brought shame to the family.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I wake up tired even after sleeping 10 hours. I sit in front of books but nothing goes into my head. My tuition teacher said I seem very distracted these days. I used to top in school but now I cannot concentrate even for 10 minutes.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I eat food only when mother calls repeatedly. I have lost interest in my favourite foods also. Sleep is disturbed, I keep thinking about future and getting very dark thoughts at night that scare me.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I don't talk to my parents much. I feel they are disappointed in me. My best friend also got into IIT and I feel very inferior next to him. I stopped going for cricket practice which I used to love so much.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I used to be so excited about learning. Now I just do things mechanically. My drawing teacher noticed I haven't been drawing for months. I feel like whatever I do is not enough and there is no point trying.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I was transferred from Mumbai to a small town for my government job. I stay alone in a government quarter. No friends, no family nearby. The town is very different from what I am used to. I feel completely isolated and don't see this improving for years.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Going to office feels like torture. I come back, cook something basic, and just sit staring at walls. Weekends are the worst because there is nothing to do and no one to talk to. I have stopped caring about my own health.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I eat maggi or bread most days because no motivation to cook properly. I have gained weight from stress eating late at night. Sleep comes after 2 AM usually and I feel groggy all morning.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I am irritable in office and my colleagues find me unfriendly. I video call parents daily but I lie that everything is fine. I don't want them to worry but the loneliness is crushing me slowly from inside.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I used to read books, had many life plans. Now I just scroll social media and feel worse seeing others enjoying life. I applied for transfer but it was rejected. I feel completely trapped with no way out.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: My husband drinks alcohol daily and becomes abusive at night. I cannot leave because of children and society will judge harshly. I feel completely trapped in this situation. I have been suffering silently for years and now feel there is no escape.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I do all household work but like a robot, with no feeling. I have forgotten what happiness feels like. I complete all tasks mechanically. By evening I feel completely empty inside with no sense of self.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I stay awake till husband comes home because I am scared. Sleep is very little, maybe 4-5 hours broken. I have lost a lot of weight. My appetite is completely gone. Neighbours have noticed I look very unwell.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I hide everything from my parents so they don't worry. My children see my sadness and ask questions I cannot answer honestly. I avoid going to relatives because I would have to pretend to be happy which exhausts me.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I used to paint, had many personal dreams. All that feels like another person's life now. I just pray each day passes without incident. I have no personal goals or motivation left for myself at all.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I am a daily wage worker and for two months did not get regular work. Family has no savings at all. Wife is sick. I could not afford her medicines. I felt like God has abandoned us completely. I thought my family would be better without me.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I feel very weak even in mornings. Going to look for work feels like a heavy burden. I walk long distances with no result. Come home with nothing and have to face the family. It is very difficult to face them every day.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: We eat only one proper meal a day. Sleep I cannot have because worries keep me awake all night. I have become very thin and weak. My whole body feels pain all the time now from stress and poor nutrition.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: My children stopped going to school because I cannot pay fees. Wife cries daily. I feel I have failed completely as a father and husband. I avoid talking to neighbours who ask about work because of shame.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: Earlier I used to work very hard and believed things will improve. Now I don't believe anything will ever change for us. I feel very dark inside most of the time. Some days I just sit and stare and don't want to do anything.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I lost my father to COVID two years back and since then I cannot recover from the grief. I was extremely close to him. I see his chair empty at the dining table and break down. I feel deeply guilty I was not there with him in hospital during his last moments because of lockdown.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I go through the motions of my day but without any feeling or purpose. I do my work but colleagues say I always seem absent-minded. On weekends I just stay in bed all day. No motivation to do anything beyond the absolute minimum.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Every morning I wake up and for a few seconds forget he is gone, then it hits me again. This repeating painful feeling is so exhausting. Appetite is very less now. I have stopped cooking elaborate meals that I used to enjoy making.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: My mother is also grieving and I should support her but I feel completely unable to. I have pushed my friends away over these two years. I don't attend any social events. I feel like I am failing everyone around me.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I used to have five year plans and ambitious goals. Now I cannot plan beyond today. The future feels grey and completely pointless. Even small things that used to make me happy mean nothing now. I wonder if I will ever feel normal again.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I am 58 years old and was made redundant from my private sector job last year. At this age finding new job is nearly impossible. My savings will last one year maybe. I feel humiliated and discarded like I have no value or worth anymore.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I sit at home all day while my wife goes to work as a school teacher. The role reversal makes me feel ashamed constantly. I was always the provider. Now I cannot even bring myself to do household work properly. I just sit doing nothing.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I sleep too much, sometimes fourteen to fifteen hours. My wife says I need to wake up but there is nothing to wake up for that feels meaningful. Food I eat when served but have no appetite at all. I have lost interest in even my favourite items.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I cannot face my son's question about what I am doing nowadays. I have cut off contact with most friends who are still working. My wife tries to support me but I push her away. I feel like a burden to everyone.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I have no motivation whatsoever. I started applying for jobs initially but after many rejections I have completely stopped. I don't see any future for myself. I sometimes think everyone in my life would be better off without me around.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: When my project deadline was extended suddenly I was stressed for a few days. But I discussed it with my manager and made a new plan. These things happen in work life, I try not to take them too personally.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: My energy is generally good. I go for a morning walk, finish office work, spend evening with family. Occasionally I feel tired but a good sleep fixes it. I manage my routine well most of the time without any issues.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: My sleep and appetite are quite normal. I sleep around 7-8 hours. I enjoy my food, my wife is a good cook. No major changes. Sometimes I eat less when very busy but nothing that concerns me.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I have good relations with family and colleagues. I attend family functions, celebrate festivals properly. Responsibilities at home and work I handle well. I communicate openly when something is bothering me.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel motivated, I recently enrolled in an online course for skill development. I have clear goals. Some days are better than others but overall I feel positive about my life and the future ahead.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: During my mother's hospitalisation I was worried and stressed, managed everything along with office work. It was tiring but I handled it well. Now she is better and I feel relieved. Such challenges are simply part of life.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Usually I am quite energetic. I do yoga in the morning, manage home and part-time tuitions. I feel tired by evening but that is normal for my busy schedule. I don't feel persistently drained or demotivated.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: During mother's illness I slept less but that was situational and temporary. Generally I sleep well. I eat home-cooked food regularly. No unusual or concerning changes in appetite or sleep patterns.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: My family relations are warm and close. We have dinner together, discuss problems openly. At work I am considered reliable and dependable. I can balance responsibilities without feeling overwhelmed for long periods.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel fairly motivated in life. I enjoy teaching children and find it deeply rewarding. I have small goals like saving for a family trip to Goa next year. Life has challenges but I generally feel okay and hopeful.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I had a fight with my roommate in hostel about cleanliness. I was upset for a day or two. Then we talked it out and things are completely fine now. Normal hostel life issues, nothing very serious at all.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I attend all my lectures, do assignments on time, go for football practice every evening. My energy is good throughout the day. Exam time is hectic but I manage well. Energy has not affected my routine negatively.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Hostel food is not great but I eat properly every meal. Sleep is good, sometimes I stay up late for studies before exams but then recover quickly. No changes that concern me at all.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I have a very good friend circle in hostel. We hang out on weekends and play games. I call home every 2-3 days. I am doing okay in academics. All relations and responsibilities are managed well.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I am very excited about upcoming internship applications. I am preparing well and feeling confident. I feel motivated and positive about the future. Normal stress about placements but that is normal for final year.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I had a difficult period adjusting to a new city after marriage. First few months were tough, missing my family back home. But slowly I made friends and explored the city. Now I feel settled and genuinely happy here.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I manage home and my part-time online work both comfortably. I have energy for socializing on weekends too. Occasionally tired but nothing that lasts more than a day or disrupts my normal routine.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Normal sleep and healthy eating habits. I enjoy cooking new recipes for my husband. Sleep is usually 7-8 hours. No particular stress affecting these basic functions of my life.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: My relationship with husband is very good. We communicate openly and well. I am building new friendships in this city. I call my parents regularly. Managing all responsibilities without any significant issues.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel genuinely positive about my life. I am taking embroidery classes and have new interests developing. I look forward to weekends and festivals. Overall I feel content and motivated. Life is good right now.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I was stressed about my 10th board exams. The pressure from school and parents was a lot. But I prepared systematically, gave my best effort. Results came out well. I learned that consistent hard work pays off in the end.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I go to school, play cricket in the evening, study at night. My energy is good throughout. Sometimes feel sleepy in afternoon class but that is very normal. Overall my daily routine is going very well.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I sleep and eat normally. Mom makes very good food. I eat everything she cooks. Sleep is 8 hours, sometimes more on Sundays. Exam time I slept less but that was temporary and I recovered quickly.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: Good relationship with parents and friends at school. I do my household chores and study regularly. Nothing has negatively affected my relationships or any of my responsibilities.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I am motivated and excited about future studies. I want to become an engineer and am preparing well for it. I have good friends and genuinely enjoy my life. I feel energetic and positive about what is ahead.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: My auto-rickshaw had a mechanical breakdown and I lost income for a week. It was financially stressful. But I borrowed from my brother, got it repaired quickly, and got back to work. Difficult week but completely manageable.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I work 10-12 hours daily as an auto driver. I am physically tired at the end of the day but feel satisfied with my work. I eat well, sleep well. My energy is good for my age and work demands.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: My wife cooks good food and I eat properly every day. Sleep is deep and good after a hard day's work. No changes in sleep or eating that would worry me at all.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: Family life is good. Children go to school, wife manages home well. I provide for family and feel proud of this. Good relationships with my regular customers also. No major issues in any area.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel motivated to save for my daughter's higher education. I have a clear purpose in my work. Hard work gives me real satisfaction every day. I feel healthy and positive. Nothing has significantly changed in my motivation.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: During Diwali we had a big family gathering and managing guests, cooking, cleaning all at once was overwhelming for a few days. But I enjoy these occasions too. After it was over I felt satisfied and happy, not hopeless.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: My energy is good for a 45-year-old I think. I manage household work, look after in-laws, and also do my part-time accounting work from home. Busy schedule but I manage without feeling completely broken down.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Sleep and eating are both fine and stable. I cook fresh food daily and eat with the family. Sleep is 6-7 hours, sometimes 8 on weekends. No change in these patterns over recent months.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: Family relations are good overall. My husband is very supportive. Children are doing well in school. I do have disagreements with mother-in-law sometimes but that is quite normal in Indian joint families.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel motivated in my daily life. I started learning computer for accounting work which I enjoy. I have family goals and personal goals. Life is busy but fulfilling. I do not feel hopeless or unmotivated.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: My elderly mother-in-law had a fall and required care for two months. As the daughter-in-law I managed most of it along with my job. I was exhausted and sometimes resentful. But she recovered and I feel it was the right thing.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I am a working mother and energy management is a constant practice. I have learned to pace myself well over the years. I take rest when I can get it. I have a routine that works for me most of the time.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Sleep and eating are stable. I make sure to eat properly because I know I cannot function otherwise. Sleep is adequate. I prioritise these basics even when my schedule is very busy.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I maintain strong relationships with family and friends. I have a good support network around me. Work is going well. I can ask for help when needed. No significant negative impacts from whatever stress I carry.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel purposeful and motivated in my life. My children are growing well, career is progressing, health is okay. I look forward to things ahead. Challenges come but I approach them with confidence rather than despair.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I had a tough quarter at work with multiple simultaneous projects. It was genuinely overwhelming for about three weeks. But I prioritised tasks, asked team for help, and we came through successfully. I felt proud how we managed.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Energy fluctuates naturally with workload. During project crunch periods I push hard, then recover over the next few days. Overall my energy supports my daily routine well. I exercise three times a week which helps a lot.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: No significant changes in either. I am mindful about sleep hygiene. I eat reasonably balanced meals. During very stressful weeks sleep may be slightly reduced but returns to normal very quickly afterward.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: Work and family life are well balanced. My spouse and I communicate openly about everything. I make time for children despite my busy schedule. I meet all my professional responsibilities. No meaningful negative impacts.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel engaged and motivated at work and at home. I have professional development goals I am actively working towards. My personal life is fulfilling. I feel I am progressing well in all important areas of life.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I appeared for my CA Inter exams and was nervous about results. I had prepared well but you never know. When results came and I passed I felt very happy and relieved. The stress before was normal exam pressure, not hopelessness.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: During exam preparation I was very focused and energetic. Now in articleship training I manage the workload well. I feel tired some evenings but motivated overall. My energy levels support my current phase of life well.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Sleep and eating were slightly disrupted during exam month but quickly normalised after results. Currently both are stable and healthy. I eat home food every day. Sleep around 7 hours which feels sufficient for me.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: Good relationships with family and colleagues in my firm. I meet all my training commitments. I spend quality time with family on weekends. Nothing has negatively impacted my relationships or responsibilities recently.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: Feeling very motivated after passing my exam. I have clear goals for my CA Final and career afterward. I look forward to growing professionally. Overall very positive energy and good motivation in my life right now.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: Last month my second UPSC attempt results came and again I did not clear prelims. Everyone in my family group on WhatsApp was asking what happened and I felt very ashamed. My parents invested so many years and coaching fees also. That day I felt like nothing in my life will move forward only.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Earlier I used to wake up at 6 and study properly. Now I wake up late and even getting out of bed feels like a big task. Whole day I feel heavy in my body like no battery is left. Even small things like going to the library feel too much.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: My sleep timing is completely disturbed. Some nights I am awake till 3 or 4 just scrolling my phone without reason. Other days I sleep too much but still feel tired. Eating also reduced, my mother keeps telling me to eat but I have no interest.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I stopped meeting my friends from the coaching class. When relatives ask about studies I avoid calls. My parents think I am being lazy but actually I feel blank inside. Because of that even household responsibilities I am not doing properly.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: Before I had motivation that next attempt I will clear IAS exam. Now even opening the books feels pointless. I sit with the book but mind is somewhere else. Sometimes I think maybe I am not capable of anything.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: My husband lost his job in a small textile factory in Surat. We already have EMI for our house and school fees for two children. Every day relatives ask what plan we have. I feel like everything is collapsing on our family.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Earlier I used to manage housework and even help children with homework. Now I feel drained from morning itself. Cooking, cleaning, everything feels very slow. By afternoon I just want to lie down.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: I wake up many times in the night thinking about money problems. Sometimes I don't feel hungry the whole day. Other days I just keep drinking tea and nothing else. My weight also reduced recently.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I become irritated with my children even for small things. My husband says I should stay positive but I cannot explain how heavy my mind feels. I stopped visiting neighbors or attending society gatherings. I just keep to myself.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: Motivation is almost gone now. Earlier I used to plan things for children like tuition and activities. Now I feel like whatever we plan will fail only. Even getting dressed to go outside feels pointless.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I work in an IT company in Hyderabad and recently my team was laid off. Somehow I am still on the bench but every week new people are removed. I keep thinking my turn will come next. That fear makes me feel very helpless.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: My routine became very dull. I log into work but I cannot focus on coding properly. After office I just lie on the bed instead of doing anything productive. Even gym which I liked earlier I stopped going.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Sleep is very light nowadays. Even small notification sound wakes me up. Eating has also become irregular because sometimes I skip meals completely. Other times I just order random food on Swiggy late at night.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: My parents keep asking about marriage plans but I avoid that topic. I also stopped talking properly with my friends. When they plan cricket or outings I say I am busy. Actually I just don't feel like going.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: I feel like my motivation has dropped to zero. Even learning new tech skills feels useless because I think layoffs will happen anyway. I start something but leave it halfway. My mind keeps telling me nothing will improve.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: After my husband passed away two years ago, I have been staying with my son's family in Nagpur. Recently there was a small argument in the house about expenses. I suddenly felt like I am a burden on everyone. That thought keeps coming again and again.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Earlier I used to wake up early and do puja and gardening. Now I feel very slow in the morning. My body feels heavy and I sit quietly most of the time. Even walking in the colony garden feels tiring.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: My sleep became very broken. I wake up early around 4 and cannot sleep again. Food also I eat less because there is no appetite. My daughter-in-law keeps insisting but I just eat little.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I stopped talking much with neighbors. Earlier we used to sit and chat in the evening. Now I prefer staying inside my room. I feel like people are just tolerating me.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: My motivation to do anything has reduced. Even festivals like Diwali or Navratri do not excite me now. Family celebrates but I feel disconnected. It feels like life is just passing slowly.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: I am a NEET aspirant from Kota. Last mock test I scored very low even after studying day and night. My parents have big expectations that I will become a doctor. I felt like I disappointed everyone.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: Earlier I used to attend all classes and revise notes. Now I skip some lectures because I feel too tired mentally. Even when sitting in class my mind feels blank. After hostel dinner I just lie down.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: Sleep is not proper at all. Some nights I keep thinking about exam questions again and again. Appetite also reduced, sometimes I skip mess food completely. Friends notice but I say I am fine.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I stopped calling my parents regularly because I feel guilty. My roommates go out for chai but I stay back. Group study also I avoid because I feel slower than others. Slowly I am isolating myself.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: Motivation that I had in 11th standard is gone now. I still open books but inside I feel hopeless. Sometimes I stare at the page without understanding anything. It feels like all effort is useless.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.
responses.answer: Our small grocery shop in the village is not running well after a big supermarket opened nearby. Customers reduced a lot. I worry about how we will pay loan we took for renovating the shop. That pressure makes me feel very low.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?
responses.answer: I still open the shop but my energy is very low. Earlier I used to talk happily with customers. Now I sit quietly most of the day. Even arranging items on shelves feels tiring.

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.
responses.answer: At night I keep thinking about business losses. Sleep comes very late. Food also I eat less because stress is always there. My wife says I look weak these days.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?
responses.answer: I become silent at home also. Children ask me to play cricket but I say later. My wife says I am not the same person as before. I feel guilty but cannot change my mood.

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.
responses.answer: Earlier I had many plans to expand the shop. Now motivation is gone. I just do basic work to keep things running. Inside I feel like whatever I try will fail.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?

responses.question: Describe any changes you have noticed in your sleep or eating patterns.

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.

responses.question: Describe a recent situation that made you feel overwhelmed or hopeless.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?

responses.question: How have these feelings affected your relationships or responsibilities?

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.

responses.question: How has your energy level affected your daily routine?

responses.question: Describe how your energy levels and motivation have changed recently.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I worry about everything — job security, whether my parents health is okay, if my EMI will bounce, what my boss thinks of my work, whether I said something wrong to my colleague. Even small things like whether I locked the door before leaving, I keep worrying the whole way to office.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: Even on Sunday I cannot relax properly. Family is watching TV and I am thinking about Monday meetings in my head. I went for a holiday to Manali last year but spent most of the time worrying about office emails. I can never fully enjoy any moment.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I avoid crowded markets, local train during peak hours, social gatherings with unknown people. I make excuses to skip company parties because I get very uncomfortable with new people. I prefer to order food online rather than go to a restaurant alone.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I overthink every decision completely. Even choosing between two job offers took me weeks and I still feel I made wrong choice. I second-guess my work constantly before submitting anything. My confidence is very low because of all this.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: My anxious thoughts never fully go away at all. I try deep breathing but after a while the thoughts come back stronger. I sometimes take half a sleeping tablet without doctor advice just to calm down. I don't know how to properly cope.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I am a mother of two school-going children and I worry about their safety every minute they are not at home. I also worry about my husband's health, rising prices, children's future, my aging parents in another city. The list is endless and I cannot stop my mind from running.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: When children are at school I should rest but I keep checking phone for any messages from school. I cannot sleep in the afternoon even when very tired. I cannot enjoy reading or TV because my mind keeps wandering to various worries.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I have stopped sending children by school bus after reading about an accident in news. I drop and pick them myself every day. I avoid going out in evenings alone. I avoid reading news completely because it increases my anxiety a lot.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I take very long to make any decision at all. Husband gets frustrated saying I overthink everything. Whether to admit children in which school, which doctor to consult — I research for weeks and still feel very uncertain and confused.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: Anxious thoughts are there from morning to night every day. I recite hanuman chalisa which sometimes helps briefly. I call my mother frequently when anxious. But at night lying in bed, the worries always come back very strongly.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I have presentations every week at work and I spend more time worrying about them than actually preparing content. I worry my voice will shake, I will forget important points, my manager will think I am incompetent. Even a week before the presentation I cannot sleep properly.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: After office I should relax but I keep replaying all the meetings in my head. Did I say something wrong? Did the client seem unhappy with me? I cannot fully switch off from work anxiety even on weekends or holidays.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I avoid volunteering for anything that requires public speaking or leading a team. I have refused promotions twice because the new role required more client interaction. I know this is badly affecting my career but I cannot help it.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: My confidence is very poor in professional settings. I feel I am not as good as my colleagues. Even though I get decent performance reviews, I feel like a fraud every day. I always think one day they will find out I am not actually competent.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: Before any presentation my heart beats very fast, palms sweat, stomach gets upset. It can last for days before the event. I take antacids regularly. Sometimes I call in sick to avoid the presentation entirely. It is getting worse over time.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I just started my first job after graduation. I worry about making mistakes, being judged negatively, getting fired. I worry if I am dressing properly, if I sent the email correctly, if I greeted the seniors right. Everything feels like a test and I am always failing.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: I work from home and cannot step away from laptop on weekends in fear of missing something important. Even during meals I check messages compulsively. My parents say I have changed since starting this job, become very tense and serious.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I avoid speaking on team calls unless directly asked by name. I strongly prefer to send messages over speaking. I avoid going to office pantry during busy hours because I don't know how to make small talk with senior colleagues.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I review every email at least five to six times before sending it. I ask my roommate to read my messages to check if they sound okay. I feel completely paralysed when asked to make independent decisions at work.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: The anxiety is almost constant during all work days. Weekends are slightly better but Sunday evening it starts again thinking about Monday. I have been having frequent headaches lately. I am honestly not coping well at all.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I had a heart problem two years ago. Now I worry about every single heartbeat. If heart beats slightly fast I immediately think I am having a heart attack. I go to cardiologist every month even when they say everything is perfectly fine. I Google symptoms daily which makes my anxiety much worse.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: I cannot enjoy any physical activity because I am monitoring my pulse constantly. Family wants to go trekking to Lonavala but I absolutely refuse. Even during evening puja I am checking my pulse on my smartwatch. I truly cannot relax at all.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I avoid any physical exertion completely. I avoid strongly air conditioned rooms thinking it will cause chest tightness. I avoid crowded areas, hill stations, and flights. I carry all my medicines everywhere even when doctors say I don't need them.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I took voluntary early retirement from my job because I thought work stress would definitely harm my heart. Now I deeply regret that decision but was so scared at the time. Every day I make major life decisions based on fear rather than what I actually want.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: Health worries are present from morning to night without a break. I cope by keeping all medicines within reach always. I call my son very frequently throughout the day. I pray a lot. But the anxiety about my health never fully goes away.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I worry a lot about what other people think of me at all times. If someone didn't reply to my WhatsApp message I keep thinking for hours what I did wrong. In social situations I keep analysing every word I said after going home. It is extremely tiring.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: After any social event I spend hours going over what I said and imagining how people judged me negatively. I cannot enjoy any happy memory of a gathering because I am too busy mentally finding my own mistakes and flaws.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I avoid family functions where there will be many relatives asking questions about job and marriage. I avoid all WhatsApp groups by keeping them permanently on mute. I strongly avoid any situations where I have to meet new people.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: My confidence is very low in social situations. I change my opinion based on who I am talking to because I am scared of any disagreement. I cannot say no to people and then feel very stressed trying to fulfil all their requests.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: Social anxiety thoughts last for days after any social event. I replay conversations continuously in my head for days. I cope by isolating myself which provides temporary relief but makes me lonelier and lonelier over time. I know this is very unhealthy.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I am appearing for NEET this year. I worry about not getting a good rank, disappointing my parents who have invested so much financially and emotionally, about what I will do if I don't get into a good medical college. The worry interferes with my actual studying.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: I cannot take any break without feeling very guilty. If I rest for even one hour I think about all the chapters I have not covered yet. My parents say I should eat properly but I eat while studying and even that makes me anxious about wasting precious time.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I have stopped all social activities completely for a year now. I don't attend any festivals or functions properly. I avoid talking to relatives who ask about exam preparation. I stay in my room most of the time and have become very withdrawn.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I keep changing my study strategy out of anxiety — one week I follow one coaching institute's method, next week I switch to another's. I cannot commit to one clear plan. My mock test scores are actually suffering because of this constant inconsistency.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: Anxiety about NEET is constant, literally 24 hours a day throughout the year. I have panic attacks during mock tests where my mind goes completely blank. I cope by studying more but this creates a cycle of complete exhaustion and even more anxiety.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: My son is studying engineering in another state and I worry non-stop about him. Did he eat properly today, is he safe, is he studying or wasting time, what if he gets sick and there is no one to care. I call him four to five times a day and he has started getting irritated with me.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: When my son doesn't reply quickly to my calls I immediately imagine the worst scenarios — accident, illness, bad company. I cannot watch TV properly when he hasn't returned my call. My husband says I am being completely excessive but I honestly cannot help it.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I avoid attending social events when son hasn't called that day. I avoid hearing any news about student unrest or hostel problems. I secretly installed a tracking application on his phone without telling him because the uncertainty is unbearable for me.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I am seriously considering asking him to transfer to a local college even though it is academically much weaker. I know rationally this is wrong for his future but the anxiety about the physical distance is completely affecting my judgment.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: The worry about my son is daily and constant throughout the day. I cope by praying and calling him repeatedly. I fast on Tuesdays for his wellbeing. My husband says I need professional counselling but I have not taken this step yet.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I have normal concerns like anyone — about work deadlines, children's studies, household finances. I think about these things but they don't overwhelm me. I address concerns when they arise and don't dwell on them unnecessarily.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: I can relax well during free time. Evenings I play with children, watch cricket, read a book. I can fully enjoy family trips and outings without work thoughts intruding. Work worries stay at work mostly. I disconnect well.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I don't avoid anything specific because of anxiety. I travel freely, attend social events, manage crowds without problems. Occasionally I prefer quieter settings but that is personal preference not anxiety-driven avoidance behavior.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I am reasonably confident in my decisions. I consider pros and cons without excessive overthinking and then decide. I trust my own judgment. I am comfortable speaking in meetings and taking on responsibilities at work.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: When I am worried about something I deal with it directly or talk to my wife about it. The worry usually passes once the situation is resolved or I have a plan. I don't experience prolonged anxiety or need special coping strategies.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: I worry about my Class 12 exams and which college I will get into. This is completely normal for my age I think. I discuss my worries with friends and parents. The worry actually motivates me to study harder rather than paralysing me.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: After studying I can enjoy playing video games or watching YouTube completely. I go out with friends on weekends and fully enjoy myself. Exam worries don't follow me everywhere. I can easily switch off and have fun.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I don't avoid any particular situation because of worry. I participate in all school events, sports day, cultural programs. I get occasionally nervous before competitions but that passes very quickly and I enjoy participating.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I am fairly confident in myself. I participate in class discussions, answer teachers' questions, take part in debates. I make decisions about my studies and extra activities without overthinking them excessively.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: If I am nervous about an upcoming exam I think about it for a day then get back to focused preparation. Worry does not last very long. I cope by talking to friends or playing cricket. Things feel fine again quite quickly.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: Running a small grocery shop has its normal worries — supplier prices, competition from big online platforms. But I have been in this business many years and handle these challenges practically. I don't lie awake worrying about them.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: Every evening I close the shop and spend good quality time with family. Friday evenings we often go to a relative's home nearby. I enjoy these breaks fully. Business worries don't usually come home with me at all.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I go to wholesale market, bank, government offices, everywhere that is required for my business. I interact with many different people daily without any anxiety. I attend community meetings and speak freely. No avoidance behaviors due to worry.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I make business decisions confidently based on my experience over years. Sometimes I take calculated risks that pay off. I am generally decisive and do not second-guess myself excessively. I am comfortable with normal business uncertainty.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: If there is a business problem I think about the solution and implement it. Worry lasts only until I have a clear plan. I talk to my wife or brother for advice when needed. I don't experience prolonged or overwhelming worry.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.
responses.answer: As a senior manager I have many professional responsibilities and naturally think about project timelines and team performance. These are work concerns I address through planning. Worry does not dominate my thinking or follow me into personal time.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
responses.answer: I have developed good work-life boundaries over many years. On vacation I genuinely disconnect and enjoy myself. Weekends I pursue my hobby of photography. I can be fully present with family without work anxiety bleeding into personal time.

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?
responses.answer: I don't avoid any situations due to anxiety. I travel frequently for both work and leisure. I enjoy social events and public speaking. Crowds and unknown situations do not cause me any unusual distress or avoidance.

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: I am confident in both professional and personal decisions. I seek input from relevant people when needed but am not paralysed by indecision. I am comfortable with uncertainty and organizational change. Anxiety does not impact my confidence.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: Any worry I experience passes naturally once I take appropriate action. Regular exercise and reading help me maintain good mental balance. I rarely experience prolonged or distressing anxiety. Life feels manageable and generally very positive.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?

responses.question: What situations or places do you avoid because of anxiety?

responses.question: Describe how anxiety affects your decision-making or confidence.
responses.answer: Umm I make decisions based on common sense and experience. No paralysis or overthinking. I am generally decisive.

responses.question: Describe how long your anxious thoughts usually last and how you cope with them.
responses.answer: Hmm worries are usually resolved same day or next day. I talk to my brother or do some exercise. Works fine.

responses.question: Describe the types of things you worry about most in your daily life.

responses.question: How does worry affect your ability to relax or enjoy free time?
